architecture_summary: PIXART-α is a Transformer-based text-to-image diffusion model built on Diffusion Transformer (DiT), with cross-attention modules for text conditioning, an adaLN-single re-parameterization for time conditioning, and a three-stage training strategy that decomposes pixel dependency learning, text-image alignment learning, and high-aesthetic high-resolution generation.

novel_contributions: PIXART-α, a Transformer-based T2I diffusion model with competitive image generation quality and low training cost
Training strategy decomposition into pixel dependency learning, text-image alignment learning, and high-aesthetic high-resolution generation
Efficient T2I Transformer design with cross-attention modules and adaLN-single re-parameterization
High-informative data construction using LLaVA auto-labeling on SAM and other datasets
Near-commercial application standards with substantially reduced training time, cost, and CO2 emissions

key_claims.claim: PIXART-α is competitive with state-of-the-art image generators and supports high-resolution image synthesis up to 1024 × 1024 with low training cost.
PIXART-α only takes 12% of Stable Diffusion v1.5's training time (∼ 753 vs. ∼ 6,250 A100 GPU days), saving nearly $300,000 and reducing 90% CO2 emissions.
Compared to RAPHAEL, PIXART-α's training cost is merely 1%.
PIXART-α achieves a FID score of 7.32 on MSCOCO zero-shot evaluation while using 753 A100 GPU days and 25M images.
PIXART-α exhibited outstanding performance across nearly all (5/6) evaluation metrics on T2I-CompBench.
The model is trained in three stages that separately optimize pixel dependency, text-image alignment, and high-resolution aesthetic generation.
The final model is trained on 64 V100 for approximately 26 days.
The paper claims PIXART-α can be combined with DreamBooth and ControlNet for customized extension.
